macos: python3 -m pip install --user pipx || brew install pipx

tools.notes: FETCH role — unified internet-access CLI; auto-fallback backends; zero mandatory API fees. Returns raw content ingested as secondary blobs. Credentials local-only.

healthCmd.windows: py -3 -c "import subprocess,sys; r=subprocess.run(['npx','-y','skills','list','-g'],capture_output=True); sys.exit(0 if b'last30days' in r.stdout.lower() else 1)"
healthCmd.macos: python3 -c "import subprocess,sys; r=subprocess.run(['npx','-y','skills','list','-g'],capture_output=True); sys.exit(0 if b'last30days' in r.stdout.lower() else 1)"
healthCmd.linux: python3 -c "import subprocess,sys; r=subprocess.run(['npx','-y','skills','list','-g'],capture_output=True); sys.exit(0 if b'last30days' in r.stdout.lower() else 1)"

tools.notes: DISCOVERY role — recency-focused (last-30-days) multi-platform synthesizer. Use for LEADS ONLY: mine its cited sources / hot threads, then fetch those underlying sources as raw blobs and chase to primary. NEVER ingest its synthesized brief as a blob (charter Part 37: gatherers return raw material only). Free core (Reddit/HN/Polymarket/GitHub/X-via-cookies); optional keys (Brave/Perplexity/ScrapeCreators/etc). Install: '/plugin marketplace add mvanhorn/last30days-skill' or 'npx skills add mvanhorn/last30days-skill -g'. healthCmd is an OS-keyed probe resolved by web-reach-ensure, not an install-relative file path.
